title: Drake Drops Surprise Double Album — Breaks Spotify Records Within 24 Hours
excerpt: Drake has done it again, releasing a surprise double album with 24 tracks at midnight that amassed over 200 million streams on Spotify within its first 24 hours, setting a new all-time global record.
content:
The rap game has been shaken once again. Drake released a surprise double album at midnight, dropping a staggering 24 tracks with zero prior announcement — his signature style of keeping the music world on its toes. Within 24 hours, the project amassed over 200 million streams on Spotify, shattering the platform's previous record and solidifying Drake's position as one of the most dominant forces in modern music.

Album Details

The double album, split into two distinct halves — Part I: North and Part II: 6God Rising — showcases the Toronto superstar at his most versatile. The first half is introspective and melodic, featuring lush production from longtime collaborator Noah "40" Shebib, while the second half is aggressive and rap-forward, produced primarily by Metro Boomin and Murda Beatz.

The guest list reads like a who's-who of contemporary music: SZA, Future, Lil Baby, Popcaan, Tems, Fridayy, and surprise appearances from both Jay-Z and Canadian R&B act The Weeknd — the latter being a long-awaited reunion that sent fans into meltdown mode.

Critical Praise

Music critics, who have had mixed reviews of some of Drake's recent work, appear largely united in their praise of the new project. Pitchfork called it "his most cohesive and emotionally resonant body of work in years," awarding it an 8.7/10. Complex declared it "Album of the Year, already, in June."

Rolling Stone's review praised the album's ambition: "Drake has always been a student of the game, and on this double record, it shows. He's studying himself, questioning his legacy, and arriving at something genuinely moving."

Billboard Chart Domination

Preliminary Billboard data suggests the album will debut at #1 on the Billboard 200, while an estimated 18 of the 24 tracks are projected to enter the Hot 100 — a feat only Drake himself has previously achieved. The lead single "Toronto Forever" is already at #1 on Apple Music in 42 countries.

Drake has yet to comment publicly on the album, maintaining radio silence on all social media platforms — letting the music speak for itself, as he has done with his biggest releases in the past.

author: StarScoop Daily Staff
metaDescription: Drake drops surprise double album with 24 tracks — 200M Spotify streams in 24 hours, The Weeknd reunion, and critical acclaim. Full review and details.
